Empires & Puzzles Mica Review: The Ninja of Silent Screams
Mica isn't your typical assassin. While other Ninjas slice and dice, he protects and accelerates. Discover why this Holy support hero might be the battery your team needs, despite his aging stats.
Mica: The Ninja of Silent Screams - Support, Survival, and Speed

Mica is the "pacifist" of the Ninja family. While his brethren Cobalt, Onyx, and Jade are busy slicing through enemies or dodging attacks, Mica stands firm, connecting his allies' souls and accelerating their potential. Released back in late 2020 as part of the Ninja Tower event, he introduced a pure support kit in a family known for aggression.
mana speed mechanic, meaning he can fire at 1x, 2x, or 3x mana. Unlike most heroes who want to kill you, Mica wants to make sure
friends kill you faster—and stay alive while doing it.

Mica’s design is a striking mix of traditional shinobi garb and radiant holy energy. Clad in golden-yellow armor with a masked face, he exudes a sense of disciplined mystery. He dual-wields kamas (sickles), yet his special skill is entirely non-offensive, which is a hilarious irony—he’s dressed to kill but prepared to hug (spiritually, via soul connection).
Let’s be real—Mica is showing his age. With a base power of
, he is significantly behind modern heroes who regularly push 1300+ Power and 1600+ stats. Even with Limit Breaks (LB2), his stats (883/1013/1701) are barely catching up to the

, which is his saving grace. The Paladin class talent gives him a chance to boost his defense further when attacked, helping him survive just long enough to fire that critical first charge. He is not a tank by modern standards; he is a utility support who needs protection to do his job.

The real value of Mica lies in his mana generation buff. Let's break down why that +24% (1st Charge) is a big deal.

is massive. Combined with Damage Share, an incoming sniper hit that would normally deal 1500 damage to one target might be reduced to ~800 total damage spread across 5 heroes (160 each). This turns lethal strikes into tickle damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family bonus is excellent.
is one of the best mechanics in the game for survival, and the
adds passive damage. Since Mica doesn't hit, this family bonus is his only way of dealing damage on his own (via counterattack).
fits him perfectly. Since his stats are outdated, reducing special skill damage received helps ensure he lives long enough to cast his buffs.

When released, Mica was often considered the "runt" of the Ninja litter compared to the destructive power of Cobalt and Onyx. However, high-level players quickly realized his potential in "Mana Engine" teams, pairing him with Xnolphod or Ludwig to create infinite loops of special skills.
Stats-wise, Mica has been crushed by power creep. A modern 3-star hero almost rivals his base durability. However,
is a mechanic that never ages. A +24% mana boost is just as valuable today as it was in 2020—perhaps even more so, as it helps you fire your modern nukes before the enemy fires theirs.
Mica is no longer a defense team candidate (too squishy, AI plays him poorly). However, on

, specifically in manual raids or wars, he is a tactical nuke of utility. If you can protect him for 5 tiles, he accelerates your entire team. He is a "Win More" hero—if you get his engine running, you dominate. If he dies early, he does nothing.
Maximizes his outdated defense stat. Every point of survivability is crucial to ensure he fires at least once.
His tile damage will be negligible.
Mica has no offensive skill. Attack stats only help his tile damage (and counterattack from family bonus). You need him alive.
. Ignore attack nodes unless they are unavoidable.
One of the few heroes who can significantly boost team mana speed for a very low tile cost (5 tiles).
Can be used as a quick pick-me-up (1x) or a massive fortress (3x) depending on the board.
Damage share completely neutralizes single-target nukers.
Pairs incredibly well with other Ninjas and Slow heroes.
Extremely fragile by modern standards. Needs Limit Breaking and Emblems to survive even basic attacks.
All his effects (Def Up, Mana Gen, Share Damage) can be dispelled. A single dispeller ruins his day.
He doesn't help you kill things directly. If your hitters are dead, Mica just prolongs your inevitable defeat.
The AI is notoriously bad at managing Charge heroes, often firing at 1x when 2x would be better, or holding for 3x and dying.
Paradoxically good. While everyone is Very Fast, his buffs make your team
Excellent for hard PvE stages (like Towers). You can charge him to 3x before a boss wave to make your team unkillable.
Decent for survival against high-star Titans, and the mana gen helps keep elemental defense down active, though he occupies a slot that could be used for a damage booster.
As a support for a 3-2 or 4-1 stack, helping your slow heavy hitters fire early.

. While not terrible, Damage Share overrides the Taunt mechanic's damage redirection in some interactions, or makes the Taunt hero take less damage (which is good) but spreads it to the squishy backline (which might be bad).

If you pulled Mica early, congratulations! He will carry you through map stages. Use him to keep your under-leveled team alive. Focus on firing his 2x charge for a good balance of protection and uptime.

Mica is a luxury tool. Do not prioritize him over modern meta heroes. However, if you have him leveled, he is a fantastic "Plan B" for War attacks when you need to use your slower, B-tier heroes. Pairing him with a slow hit-all hero can make that team viable against meta defenses.
use Alpha Aethers on him unless you are a die-hard Ninja fan; the stats return isn't worth it compared to a modern carry.
“I use Mica in my mono holy team... His main job is to save your team against special skill attacks... I do not think that he needs any buff, just needs to give him a chance.”
“Mica is not a hero for defense. Mica shines on quickly keeping your team alive, and increasing their mana speed.”
Many players initially struggled to find a use for him, calling him the "weakest Ninja," but those who mastered the mana breakpoints swear by his offensive utility.
Mica is the ultimate "enabler." He doesn't win the game himself; he creates the conditions for your other heroes to win. While his stats are a relic of the past, his
skill remains one of the most versatile support abilities in Empires & Puzzles. He turns your team into a well-oiled, damage-sharing, mana-generating machine.
A valuable offensive tool for veteran rosters, but keep him away from your defense team.
Mica currently does not have a costume, but if one is released, expect it to heavily modernize his stats—which is frankly all he needs to become top-tier again.
